labs.title: CLF-C02 Lab 01: Environment Baseline
labs.environment: Guided cloud console / CLI / local simulator depending on provider access
labs.description: Create a safe learning environment with accounts/projects, budgets, naming, and least-privilege access. This standalone lab supports AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner (CLF-C02) Academy and reinforces the domain: Cloud concepts, value, and adoption.
objectives: Use IAM and EC2 in a realistic workflow.
Practice safe setup, validation, cleanup, and documentation.
Connect every task to an exam-style requirement.
Create notes that can be indexed by the Subul AI Tutor.
labs.scenario: You are supporting a team preparing for AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner (CLF-C02) Academy. The team must implement a small but production-minded workflow involving IAM, EC2, identity, monitoring, and cost/risk controls.

tasks.title: Plan the lab
tasks.instructions: Identify the domain "Cloud concepts, value, and adoption", the services involved, and two constraints that could change the design.
validationHints: A written plan names workload, identity boundary, monitoring signal, and cleanup step.

tasks.title: Implement the core workflow
tasks.instructions: Create or simulate the required IAM/EC2 resources. Prefer least privilege and documented naming.
validationHints: Resource exists or simulated output is shown; unsafe broad access is avoided.

tasks.title: Secure and monitor
tasks.instructions: Add identity/policy control and at least one operational signal such as logs, metrics, events, traces, or audit output.
validationHints: Evidence shows who can access the resource and how failures are detected.

tasks.title: Test and troubleshoot
tasks.instructions: Introduce or analyze a likely failure mode, then explain the diagnosis and fix.
validationHints: Learner can explain symptom, root cause, and corrective action.

tasks.title: Cleanup and reflect
tasks.instructions: Remove resources or document cleanup commands. Write an exam-style explanation of what the lab taught.
validationHints: Cleanup evidence plus short reflection.

metadata.aiTutorContext: CLF-C02 Cloud concepts, value, and adoption IAM EC2

labs.scenario: You are supporting a team preparing for Google Professional Cloud Architect Academy. The team must implement a small but production-minded workflow involving Cloud Spanner, Cloud Armor, identity, monitoring, and cost/risk controls.

tasks.title: Plan the lab
tasks.instructions: Identify the domain "Design security and compliance", the services involved, and two constraints that could change the design.
validationHints: A written plan names workload, identity boundary, monitoring signal, and cleanup step.

tasks.title: Implement the core workflow
tasks.instructions: Create or simulate the required Cloud Spanner/Cloud Armor resources. Prefer least privilege and documented naming.
validationHints: Resource exists or simulated output is shown; unsafe broad access is avoided.

tasks.title: Secure and monitor
tasks.instructions: Add identity/policy control and at least one operational signal such as logs, metrics, events, traces, or audit output.
validationHints: Evidence shows who can access the resource and how failures are detected.

tasks.title: Test and troubleshoot
tasks.instructions: Introduce or analyze a likely failure mode, then explain the diagnosis and fix.
validationHints: Learner can explain symptom, root cause, and corrective action.

tasks.title: Cleanup and reflect
tasks.instructions: Remove resources or document cleanup commands. Write an exam-style explanation of what the lab taught.
validationHints: Cleanup evidence plus short reflection.

labs.scenario: You are supporting a team preparing for Kubernetes and Cloud Native Associate (KCNA) Academy. The team must implement a small but production-minded workflow involving Deployments, Services, identity, monitoring, and cost/risk controls.

tasks.title: Plan the lab
tasks.instructions: Identify the domain "Kubernetes fundamentals", the services involved, and two constraints that could change the design.
validationHints: A written plan names workload, identity boundary, monitoring signal, and cleanup step.

tasks.title: Implement the core workflow
tasks.instructions: Create or simulate the required Deployments/Services resources. Prefer least privilege and documented naming.
validationHints: Resource exists or simulated output is shown; unsafe broad access is avoided.

tasks.title: Secure and monitor
tasks.instructions: Add identity/policy control and at least one operational signal such as logs, metrics, events, traces, or audit output.
validationHints: Evidence shows who can access the resource and how failures are detected.

tasks.title: Test and troubleshoot
tasks.instructions: Introduce or analyze a likely failure mode, then explain the diagnosis and fix.
validationHints: Learner can explain symptom, root cause, and corrective action.

tasks.title: Cleanup and reflect
tasks.instructions: Remove resources or document cleanup commands. Write an exam-style explanation of what the lab taught.
validationHints: Cleanup evidence plus short reflection.

labs.title: VA-003 Lab 11: Integrated Scenario
labs.environment: Guided cloud console / CLI / local simulator depending on provider access
labs.description: Combine multiple services into a practical mini architecture. This standalone lab supports HashiCorp Certified: Vault Associate (003) Academy and reinforces the domain: Authentication methods and identity.
objectives: Use Vault CLI and Auth methods in a realistic workflow.
Practice safe setup, validation, cleanup, and documentation.
Connect every task to an exam-style requirement.
Create notes that can be indexed by the Subul AI Tutor.
labs.scenario: You are supporting a team preparing for HashiCorp Certified: Vault Associate (003) Academy. The team must implement a small but production-minded workflow involving Vault CLI, Auth methods, identity, monitoring, and cost/risk controls.

tasks.title: Plan the lab
tasks.instructions: Identify the domain "Authentication methods and identity", the services involved, and two constraints that could change the design.
validationHints: A written plan names workload, identity boundary, monitoring signal, and cleanup step.

tasks.title: Implement the core workflow
tasks.instructions: Create or simulate the required Vault CLI/Auth methods resources. Prefer least privilege and documented naming.
validationHints: Resource exists or simulated output is shown; unsafe broad access is avoided.

tasks.title: Secure and monitor
tasks.instructions: Add identity/policy control and at least one operational signal such as logs, metrics, events, traces, or audit output.
validationHints: Evidence shows who can access the resource and how failures are detected.

tasks.title: Test and troubleshoot
tasks.instructions: Introduce or analyze a likely failure mode, then explain the diagnosis and fix.
validationHints: Learner can explain symptom, root cause, and corrective action.

tasks.title: Cleanup and reflect
tasks.instructions: Remove resources or document cleanup commands. Write an exam-style explanation of what the lab taught.
validationHints: Cleanup evidence plus short reflection.
